one_liner: Real wedge, real early signal, but the founders are hand-wringing about a decision they should've already made.
key_points: 8 of 12 kept using it daily past week one — that's the number that matters, not the 12. Daily usage after week one is real pull, not a novelty bump.
The founder used to BE the customer. That's worth more than any market research deck. Fastest path to paying customers is his old network, and he already has that.
Fix photo attachment first — that's the stated churn reason from real users, not a guess. Ship that before touching anything else.

The 'integrate vs replace Jobber/Housecall Pro' indecision needs to end now. Integrate. You don't have the capital or time to fight incumbent distribution — be the best voice layer that plugs into what they already pay for, and use that as the wedge into their user base.
$19/mo/tech with no free tier is the right call for this buyer — they don't shop on price, they shop on 'does this survive being dropped in a truck.' Good instinct, don't second-guess it.
